skill.name: threat-modeling-expert
skill.description: Expert in threat modeling methodologies, security architecture review, and risk assessment. Masters STRIDE, PASTA, attack trees, and security requirement extraction. Use for security architecture reviews, threat identification, and secure-by-design planning.
skill.summary: Guides structured threat modeling, security architecture review, risk prioritization, and mitigation planning.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance indicators in SKILL.md. Manual review found defensive security guidance, not cryptographic code, reconnaissance commands, network access, data collection, or execution behavior. No evidence found of prompt injection, scripts, external commands, filesystem access, or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static alerts at SKILL.md lines 3, 8, 17, 22, 44, and 60 are defensive threat-modeling text. The duplicate line 3 alert is also covered. These lines do not define cryptographic algorithms, weak cipher use, crypto configuration, or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain prose about design, mitigation, review, and threat modeling. No code, command, cryptographic primitive, or configuration setting is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 49 advises users to avoid storing sensitive details without access controls. It does not request host enumeration, network discovery, account discovery, or collection of system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a safety recommendation for handling sensitive threat-model content. There is no reconnaissance workflow or command-like instruction.

content.user_title: Review Security Architecture With Threat Modeling
content.value_statement: Teams miss security risks when architecture changes faster than documentation. This skill guides structured threat modeling with STRIDE, attack trees, risk scoring, and mitigation planning.
actual_capabilities: Applies STRIDE analysis to components, data flows, and trust boundaries.
Builds attack trees for critical user journeys and sensitive assets.
Identifies assets, entry points, threats, mitigations, and residual risks.
Maps security controls to prioritized threats and implementation needs.
Supports security documentation for design reviews and audit preparation.
limitations: Does not replace authorized penetration testing or automated vulnerability scanning.
Does not provide legal, regulatory, or compliance certification.
Requires accurate architecture, data flow, and scope information from the user.
Cannot verify deployed controls without access to implementation evidence.

use_cases.title: Review a New Product Architecture
use_cases.description: Identify trust boundaries, data flows, attack surfaces, and required mitigations before engineering work begins.

use_cases.title: Prepare for a Security Design Review
use_cases.description: Convert architecture notes into prioritized threats, control gaps, and review-ready security requirements.

use_cases.title: Teach Structured Security Thinking
use_cases.description: Guide teams through repeatable STRIDE and attack tree exercises using their own system context.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Threat Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review for a single feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic threat model for this feature. Identify assets, entry points, trust boundaries, likely threats, and practical mitigations.

prompt_templates.title: Apply STRIDE to a Data Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply STRIDE to this data flow. List threats by component, explain impact, assign priority, and recommend security requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Build Attack Trees
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced analysis for critical abuse paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build attack trees for the most critical assets in this system. Include attacker goals, preconditions, paths, controls, and residual risks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Security Review Package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced preparation for a formal design review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this architecture description into a security review package with scope, assumptions, STRIDE findings, attack trees, mitigations, owners, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Threat model a new payment checkout flow.
output_examples.output: A structured review covering cardholder data, user sessions, third-party processor calls, fraud paths, trust boundaries, prioritized STRIDE findings, and mitigation owners.

output_examples.input: Review an internal admin console before launch.
output_examples.output: A risk-focused summary of privileged actions, authorization threats, audit logging gaps, session risks, insider threats, and release-blocking controls.

output_examples.input: Prepare security requirements for a file upload service.
output_examples.output: A requirements list covering content validation, malware scanning, storage isolation, access control, logging, retention, abuse monitoring, and residual risk tracking.

best_practices: Provide system scope, data flow details, assets, users, and trust boundaries before requesting analysis.
Review findings with developers, security owners, and product stakeholders before finalizing mitigations.
Update the threat model when architecture, dependencies, data sensitivity, or deployment boundaries change.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for systems you are not authorized to review.
Do not treat generated threats as proof that controls are implemented.
Do not store sensitive architecture details in shared documents without access controls.

faq.question: What threat modeling methods does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports STRIDE, attack trees, data flow analysis, risk prioritization, mitigation planning, and security requirement extraction.

faq.question: Can it create a complete threat model from minimal context?
faq.answer: It can start one, but useful results require architecture details, assets, data flows, users, trust boundaries, and assumptions.

faq.question: Does this replace a security review by a human expert?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure analysis and documentation, but humans must validate findings, business impact, and implementation evidence.

faq.question: Can it help with audit preparation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize threats, mitigations, residual risks, assumptions, and security requirements for review discussions.

faq.question: Is it safe to include secrets or production credentials?
faq.answer: No. Provide sanitized architecture details and avoid secrets, customer data, tokens, keys, and confidential operational details.

faq.question: Which AI coding tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
